column1: # Maplify provenance census — Phase 11 / plan 11-02 (RESOLVE-02/03, D-08)
# Source: PROD (project ref grztmjpzamcxlzecmqca) via IPv4 pooler aws-1-us-west-1.pooler.supabase.com:5432, session mode
#   (direct host db.<ref>.supabase.co is IPv6-only — no route from this host; pooler used instead)
# READ-ONLY guarantee: a single SELECT, no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL. maplify.sightings.comments NOT mutated (SC#2).
# Total maplify.sightings rows at census time: 6832 (cf. EXEC-SUMMARY §3 ~6827 — minor expected drift).
# Precedence note: each row is assigned to its HIGHEST-PRECEDENCE signal (bracket -> attribution -> source-only),
#   matching the locked resolution order. So a row tagged "[Orca Network] ... Submitted by a Cascadia Trusted Observer"
#   counts under bracket 'Orca Network', NOT attribution 'Cascadia'. This is why attribution counts here are far lower
#   than EXEC-SUMMARY §3 (which counted signals independently): e.g. Cascadia=52 here vs ~2014 there. CONFIRM this is
#            WHEN comments ~ 'Trusted Observer' THEN regexp_replace(comments,'.*Submitted by an? (.+) Trusted Observer.*','\1','g')
# proposed_slug = deterministic mapping to an EXISTING public.collections slug (Phase 9 seed). Confidence:
#   HIGH  = exact slug match (bracket stub / named / attribution org).
#   HUMAN = no obvious collection; needs your decision (D-11 source-code curation) — fill target_collection_slug or STAY_NULL.
# target_collection_slug = LEFT BLANK — fill during 11-02 Task 2 sign-off (or write STAY_NULL).
